DEFINITION
Cephalometrics is defined as the analysis and interpretation of standerdised
radiographs of facial boxes using standerdised skull radiograph to access
facial, dental and skeletal relationships as well as airway analysis.
TYPES OF CEPHALOGRAM
A> Lateral cephalogram
- provides a Pateral view of skull
- X-ray beam perpendicular to patients sagiltal plane
B> Frontal Cephalogram
- Anteropasteriar view of skull
- X-ray beam perpendicular to patients caronal plane
USES OF CEPHALOGRAM
In orthodontic diagnosis and treatment planning
- Assessment of harizontal /vertical skeletal relationship
incisor position/inclination, soft tissue profile
ЙОДАИ
<1
- Orthognathic surgery
Helps in classification of skeletal and dental abnormalities
гаяо
Helps in predicting growth related changes and changes associated
with surgical treatment.
Helps in evaluation of treatment results

CEPHALOSTAT

CEPHALOMETRIC LANDMARKS
It is of 2 types
A> Anatomic Landmarks.
- These landmarks represent actual anatomic structures of skull
B> Derived Landmarks
- These are the landmarks that have been obtained secondarily
from anatomic structures in a cephalogram. GEBHATO 70 The
Ideal Requirements of Cephalometric Landmarks
It should be easily seen in a radiograph
It should be uniform in outline and should be
reproducible
The landmarks should permit valid quantitative
measurements of lines & angles projected from them
МАЯЮОЛАНЯЗ) 70 JEEFS
The important landmarks are
1> NASION - The most anterior point midway between the frontal
and nasal bones on the fronto-nasal suture.
2> ORBITALE - The lowest point on the inferior bony margin of arbit
3> PORION - The highest bony point on the upper margin of external
acoustic meatis.
4> SELLA - The point representing the midpoint of the pituitary
fassa or sella tursica
5> POINT A - It is the deepest point in the midline between anterior
nasal spine and alveolar crest between 2 central incisors.
It is also called as subspinale.
6> POINT B- It is the deepest point in the midline between the
alveolar crest of mandible and the mental. process.
It is also called supramentale.
7> BASION - It is the median point of the anterior margin of foramen
magnum.
TATEOUAH930
8> BOLTON POINT - The highest point at the past condylar notchof
the occipital bone

g> ANTERIOR NASAL SPINE - It is the anterior tip of the sharp bony
process of the maxilla in the midline of the lower margin
of anterior nasal spine.
10> GONION - It is a constructed point at the junction of ramal plane
and the mandibular plane.
11> POGONION - It is the most anterior point' of the bony chin in the
median plane
12> MENTON - It is the most inferior midline point on the mandibular
symphysis.
13> GNATHION - It is the most antero inferior point on the symphysis
of the chin. It is drawn by intersecting a Line drawn perpendicul-
-ar to the line connecting menton and pogonion.
14> ARTICULARE - It is a point at the junction of the posterior barder
of ramus and the inferior border of the basilar part of the
occipital bone.
15> CONDYLION - The most superior point on the head of condyle.
16> PROSTHION - The lowest most anteriors Dointy on that alveolar bone
in the midline, between the upper central incisors.
It is also called supradentale.
17> INFRADENTALE - The highest and most anterior point ЗИА on the
alveolar process, in the median plane between the mandibular
central incisors.
18> KEY RIDGE - The lower most point on the contour of the anterior
wall of infratemporal fossa.
19> POSTERIOR NASAL SPINE - The intersection of a continuation of
the anterior wall of the pterygopalatine fassa and the floor of
the nase.
20> PTM POINT - It is the intersection of the inferior barder of faramen
rotundum with the pasterior wall of Terygo-maxillary fissure.
21> GLABELLA - It is the most prominent point of the farhead in the
mid-sagittal plane.
22> CHELION - It is the Lateral terminus of the oral slit on the outer
corner of mouth.

23> SUBNASALE - The point where the lowest barder of the nase meets
the outer contour of the upper lip.

LATERAL CEPHALOMETRIC LANDMARKS
LINES & PLANES IN CEPHALOMETRICS
HORIZONTAL PLANES
A> SN PLANE - Between centre of sella tursica and the anterior
point of the fronto-nasal suture.
B> FRANKFORT HORIZONTAL PLANE - 1 Lowest point of arbit (orbitale,
and porion
c> OCCLUSAL PLANE - Plane bisecting the pasterior occlusion of the
permanent molars and premolars.
D> PALATAL PLANE - Anterior nasal spine to Pasterior nasal spine
of palatine bone
E> MANDIBULAR PLANE - Several mandibular planes
i> Tangent to Lower barder of mandible (Tweed)
ii> A line connecting gonion to grathion (Steiner)
iii> A line connecting gonion and menton (Downs)
F> BASION-NASION PLANE - Line connecting basion and nasion.
It represents cranial base.

VERTICAL PLANE
A> A - POG LINE - Point A on maxilla to pogonion on mandible.
B> FACIAL PLANE - Nasion to pogonion
c> FACIAL AXIS - Ptm point to gnathion

DOWNS ANALYSIS (SHORT NOTE)
First published by Downs in 1948
Most frequently used cephalometric analysis
According to Downs,
"Balance of face is determined by position of mandible"
Downs analysis consists of 10 parameters of which 5 are skeletal
and 5 are dental.
SKELETAL PARAMETERS
A> FACIAL ANGLE - Inferior angle formed by intersection of nasion-
pogonion ud plane and ЦАИА F.H. plane.
108
Average. value 87.8° [range ensola 82°-95°

Significance - indication of antero-pasteriar positioning of
mandible in relation to upper face.
- increased in skeletal class III with prominent chin
- decreased in skeletal class II
B> ANGLE OF CONVEXITY - This is formed by intersection of a line
from nasion to point A and a line from point A to pogonion
Average value 0° [ Range -8.5°-10°]
Significance - It reveals the convexity or concavity of skeletal profile
- - pasitive angle suggests a prominent maxillary
dental base in relation to mandible.
- negative angle is indicative of prognothic profile
c> A-B PLANE ANGLE - It is formed between a line connecting
point A and point B and a line joining nasion to
pogonion.
Mean value is - 4.6° [range -9-0°]
Significance - indicative of maxillo mandibular relationship
in relation to facial plane.
- negative since point B is positioned beyond A.
em positive in class III malocclusion or class I
malocclusion with mandible prominence.

Average value 21.9° [range 17°-28°]

DENTAL PARAMETERS
A> CANT OF OCCLUSAL PLANE - Angle farmed between occlusal plane
and FH plane.
Average value 9.3° [range is 1.5°14°]
Significance - gives a measure of slope of occlusal plane relative
to FH plane.
B> INTER -INCISAL ANGLE - Angle farmed between long axis of upper &
lower incisors.
Average value 135.4° [range 130°-150.5°.]
Significance - angle is decreased in class I bimaxillary protrusion
c> INCISOR OCCLUSAL PLANE ANGLE - This is the inside inferior
angle formed by intersection between long axis of Lower
central incisor and the occlusal plane.
It is read as plus or minus deviation from right angle.
Average value 14.5° [range 3.5-20°]
Significance - An increase in suggests increased lower incisor
proclination.

D> INCISOR MANDIBULAR PLANE ANGLE - It is formed by intersection
of long axis of lower incisor & mandibular plane.
Average value is 1.4° [range a -8.5°-7°]
Significance - increase in this angle suggests lower incisor proclination
E> UPPER INCISOR TO A- POG LINE - This is a Linear measurement
between the incisal edge of the maxillary central incisor
& the line joining point A to pogonion.
Average value 2.7 mm [range - 1-5 mm]
Significance — The measurement is more in patients presenting
with upper Incisor incisor proctination

STEINER ANALYSIS
SKELETAL ANALYSIS
A> S.N.A ANGLE - It is the angle farmed by intersection of S.N plane and
a line joining nasion to point A.
Average value 82°
Significance - it indicates relative antero-pasterior pasitioning of
maxilla in relation to cranial base.
- A larger than normal value indicates maxilla is
prognothic [class II]
- smaller value indicates retrognathic maxilla [class
B> S.N.B ANGLE - It is the angle between S.N plane and a line joining
nasion to point B.
Average value 80°
Significance - it indicates antero-pasterior pasitioning of mandible in
relation to cranial base.
STEINER 1930 ANALYSIS
- Increase in angle indicates prognathic mandible
[class III]
- Less than normal angle suggests retrusive mandible
[classII]
c> A.N.B ANGLE - The angle is farmed by intersection of lines joining
nasion to point A and nasion to point B.
Average value 2°
Significance - It denotes the relative position of maxilla & mandible
to each other.
- Increase in value indicates class III relationship &
decrease suggests class II relationship.
D> MANDIBULAR PLANE ANALYSIS - It is the angle formed between
SN plane and mandibular planne.
Average value 32°.
Significance - It indicates about the growth pattern of individual.
- A lower angle is indicative of harizontal growing
face and a vertical angle indicates a vertically
growing individual.

DENTAL ANALYSIS
A> UPPER INCISOR TO N-A ANGLE - It is the angle formed by the
intersection of long axis of upper central incisors and the line
joining nasion to point A.
Normal angle 22°
Significance - this angle indicates the relative inclination of upper
incisors.
- an increased angle is seen in pts. who have proclined
upper incisors as in class II division I malocchusion
B> LOWER INCISOR TO N-B ANGLE - It is formed between N-B plane
and the long axis of Lower incisor.
Mean value 25°
Significance - It indicates the inclination of lower central incisor.
- An increased value indicates proclination of Lower incisors
& a Lowered value indicates upright/retroclined lower
incisors.
c> UPPER INCISOR TO N-A LINEAR — It is a linear measurement
between labial surface of upper central incisor & the line
joining nasion to point A.
Normal value 4 mm
Significance - It helps in determining upper incisor position
- It increases in cases with proclined upper incisors.
D> LOWER INCISOR TO N-B LINEAR - It is the distance between Labial
surface of lower central incisor and the Line joining nasion
to point B.
Normal value 4 mm.
Significance - It helps in determining lower incisor inclination.
— Increase in value indicates proclined Lower incisors

WITS APPRAISAL
Wits appraisal is a measure C/C of the extent to which the maxilla and the
mandible are related to each other in the antero-posterior or sagittal
A plane. functional occlusal plane is drawn through the overlapping cusps of
1st premolars and first molars. Rd 32/pm 20.1
Perpendiculars are drawn to the occlusal plane from points A and B.
The points of contact of these perpendiculars on the occlusal plane
are termed AO and BO evoly surala
The distance between AO and BO gives the antero pasterior relation
between 2 jaws. It to all Hd bowers 2Nt &j FT
In case of males point BO is ahead of AO by 1mm, while in case of
females AO and BO coincide. will NaiTaviloNi 2N1 H
